requirement_groups.name: Foundational Data Science Courses
requirement_groups.description: Core data science and programming courses required for all DS majors.

sub_groups.name: Intro Programming
sub_groups.notes: CS 220 is the preferred path for DS; CS 300 is the CS-track equivalent.

sub_groups.name: Ethics Requirement
sub_groups.description: One ethics course: LIS 461, ECE/ISyE 570, or PHILOS 244.
sub_groups.notes: ECE/ISyE 570 also appears in the IE Industrial Data Analytics focus area — strong overlap candidate.

requirement_groups.name: Major Electives
requirement_groups.description: 18 credits of upper-level electives, at least one from each required category. At most one course per probability/inference/linear algebra grouping may count.

sub_groups.name: Linear Algebra
sub_groups.description: Choose exactly one linear algebra course.
sub_groups.notes: Only one linear algebra course may count toward the DS major. MATH 340 also required by IE.

sub_groups.name: Advanced Computing
sub_groups.description: At least one course from the advanced computing list.
sub_groups.notes: COMP SCI/ECE/ISyE 524 also in IE OR focus area. COMP SCI 400 and 412 also IE CS electives.

sub_groups.name: Statistical Modeling
sub_groups.description: At least one course from the statistical modeling list.
sub_groups.notes: STAT 311/309 also satisfies IE stats requirement. STAT 310/312 also satisfies IE stats II. ISyE 575 also in IE Applications focus. ISyE/MATH/OTM/STAT 632 also in IE OR focus.

sub_groups.name: Machine Learning
sub_groups.description: At least one course from the machine learning list.
sub_groups.notes: ISyE 521 also in IE Industrial Data Analytics focus area — key overlap.

sub_groups.name: Other Electives
sub_groups.description: Up to 2 courses from the other electives list, or additional courses from the required categories above, to reach 18 total credits.
sub_groups.notes: At most 2 courses from this specific list. Additional credits beyond 2 must come from the Advanced Computing, Statistical Modeling, or Machine Learning categories. ISyE 323, 412, 512, 612 also appear in IE core or IDA focus area.

requirement_groups.notes: Must total 18 upper-level credits. Only one probability course (MATH 331, STAT/MATH 309, STAT 311, MATH/STAT 431), one inference course (STAT/MATH 310, STAT 312), and one linear algebra course may count.

requirement_groups.name: L&S Bachelor of Science Degree Requirements
requirement_groups.description: Additional requirements for the L&S BS degree that DS students must satisfy.

sub_groups.name: L&S Mathematics Requirement
sub_groups.description: Two courses of 3+ credits at Intermediate or Advanced level in MATH, COMP SCI, or STAT. Max one COMP SCI and one STAT course.
sub_groups.notes: Satisfied by major coursework in most cases.

sub_groups.name: Language Requirement
sub_groups.description: Complete third unit of a language other than English.
sub_groups.notes: May be satisfied by AP/IB scores or placement test. Check with L&S advisor.

sub_groups.name: L&S Breadth Requirements
sub_groups.description: 12 cr humanities (6 must be literature), 12 cr social science, 12 cr natural science (6 biological + 6 physical).
sub_groups.notes: Wide range of courses satisfy each category. Overlaps significantly with university gen ed breadth requirements.

requirement_groups.notes: DS is an L&S degree. These requirements are in addition to the major. See ls_bs_degree_2025 program file when implemented.
